title: clerodendrum cephalanthum subsp. occidentale
intro: Clerodendrum cephalanthum subsp. occidentale, also known as the Western glory bower, is a plant species within the Clerodendrum genus. It is a flowering plant native to the Pacific Islands, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves, stems, and roots. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Clerodendrum cephalanthum subsp. occidentale is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its health benefits.
